definition: Resting orders — stop losses and pending orders clustered at predictable price levels.
feeds_into: draw_on_liquidity (the nearest unswept level is the DOL)
turtle_soup (turtle soup IS a liquidity sweep + reversal)
related_to: dealing_range (range high/low are the primary liquidity levels)
equal_highs_lows (engineered liquidity — a subset of liquidity)
bridge_integration: Liquidity is foundational: external liquidity (swing highs/lows, PDH/PDL/PWH/PWL/PMH/PML, equal highs/lows, session highs/lows, key opens D/W/M/Q) is mapped, internal liquidity (FVGs, OBs, breakers — see those cards) is mapped separately. (1) Liquidity map: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:522 calls analysis.liquidity.build_liquidity_map(df, swings) on the M15 trigger TF; line 581 merges H4 liquidity levels for macro DOL targeting (deduped within 0.1% price proximity, line 583-588). Each LiquidityLevel carries source ('PDH','PDL','PWH','PWL','PMH','PML','equal_highs','equal_lows','session_high','session_low','D_OPEN','W_OPEN','M_OPEN','Q_OPEN', plus swing_high/swing_low) and liquidity_type. Key opens are extracted to result.key_opens (line 524-527) for direct injection into the Claude prompt. (2) Sweep detection and significance filtering: scan_sweeps(levels, df, lookback_bars=64) at ict_pipeline.py:529 — 64 bars on M15 covers ~16h, enough to span London→NY. Lines 532-553 enforce ICT's 'must sweep a KNOWN level' rule — only sweeps of {PDH,PDL,PWH,PWL,PMH,PML,equal_highs,equal_lows,session_high,session_low,D_OPEN,W_OPEN,M_OPEN,Q_OPEN} count as significant. Minor swing sweeps are downgraded to 'minor_sweep_only' advanced_factor. result.sweep_detected and the swept-source list ('sweep_of_PDH+equal_highs', etc.) are added to advanced_factors and propagate into the Claude prompt. (3) Scoring: analysis.ict.scorer.score_ict_setup at scorer.py:266-281 awards up to 20 pts (weight['liquidity']) — 70% if a recent sweep with sweep_direction OPPOSITE to trade direction exists (BSL sweep confirms BEARISH; SSL sweep confirms BULLISH; this is the manipulation-then-reversal logic), +30% if a draw_on_liquidity target is identified. Lands on SymbolAnalysis.liquidity_score and SymbolAnalysis.sweep_detected. (4) DOL targeting: ict_pipeline.py:593 calls get_draw_on_liquidity(levels, current_price, bias=direction) to pick the nearest unswept external level in trade direction; result.dol drives the prompt's TP-target line in claude_decision.py. claude_decision.py:657-684 enforces a hard DOL pre-filter: if no fib_tp_level or key_open is >= 4x ATR away from price, the trade is SKIPped (Grade A/B exempt) — implements ICT 2022 Step 2's 'no clear DOL = no trade' rule. (5) Equal highs/lows: ict_pipeline.py:920-926 uses detect_equal_levels_clustered(swings); when price is within 1% of an equal-level cluster, advanced_factors gets 'EQ_<level_type>(<count>)'. synergy_scorer.py:120-124 _has_equal_levels detects this, feeding the 'Sweep + equal levels' synergy (+4) and 'Fib + EqLevel' (+3). (6) Reasoning post-gates: claude_decision.py:1099-1117 _REASONING_HARD_GATE_PHRASES contains the 'sweep of low/PDL/PWL/equal_lows/...' and 'sweep of high/PDH/PWH/equal_highs/...' phrase tuples; _check_opposing_sweep at line 1149 rejects BUY-after-high-sweep and SELL-after-low-sweep — implements 'trading WITH the sweep = joining manipulation'. BSL/SSL classification is implicit in the sweep_direction field on each LiquiditySweep — there is no separate BSL/SSL constant in the bridge; the directional logic is captured by 'sweep_direction != direction' at scorer.py:272.

buy_side_liquidity_BSL.definition: Stops from shorts + buy-stops from breakout traders sitting ABOVE swing highs, equal highs, resistance.
buy_side_liquidity_BSL.created_by: market_structure — every swing high creates BSL above it
buy_side_liquidity_BSL.targeted_by: liquidity_sweep when price raids above highs

sell_side_liquidity_SSL.definition: Stops from longs + sell-stops sitting BELOW swing lows, equal lows, support.
sell_side_liquidity_SSL.created_by: market_structure — every swing low creates SSL below it
sell_side_liquidity_SSL.targeted_by: liquidity_sweep when price raids below lows

external_liquidity.what: Swing highs and lows — where most stops cluster.
external_liquidity.role: TARGET — price moves TO these levels to take liquidity. These are your TP.
external_liquidity.connection: external_liquidity IS draw_on_liquidity. The TP target is always external liquidity.

internal_liquidity.what: FVGs, order blocks, breaker blocks — price inefficiencies inside the range.
internal_liquidity.role: ENTRY — you enter AT these levels after external liquidity is taken.
internal_liquidity.connection: internal_liquidity = order_blocks + fair_value_gaps + breaker_blocks. These concepts ARE internal liquidity.

equal_highs_lows.definition: Two or more swing highs/lows at nearly the same price — engineered liquidity magnets.
equal_highs_lows.connection: Equal highs/lows are a SUBSET of liquidity. They form at dealing_range boundaries or within structure. They are HIGH-PRIORITY draw_on_liquidity targets.

draw_on_liquidity.definition: The nearest unswept external liquidity in the expected direction. This is WHERE price is going.
draw_on_liquidity.depends_on: market_structure (to identify unswept swing points) + liquidity (to know which levels are unswept)
draw_on_liquidity.feeds_into: risk_management — draw_on_liquidity IS your TP target
